task_completion.description: Import the mouth shape sequence into OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface should be visible with the mouth shape sequence (mouth.0001, mouth.0002, mouth.0003) loaded in the Level Strip or Xsheet/Timeline.
The Xsheet or Timeline in OpenToonz should show the first drawing (mouth.0001) spanning from frame 1 to frame 10.
The Xsheet or Timeline in OpenToonz should show the second drawing (mouth.0002) spanning from frame 11 to frame 20.
The Xsheet or Timeline in OpenToonz should show the third drawing (mouth.0003) spanning from frame 21 to frame 30.

task_completion.description: Configure output settings to render PNG files to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog in OpenToonz should be visible, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/lipsync/' and the file format set to 'png'.
A render progress bar/completion dialog in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window showing at least 30 PNG files generated in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/lipsync/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to create the animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (e.g., dragging cells in the Xsheet, opening Output Settings) rather than relying solely on terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the software by simply copying and renaming files in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'cp mouth.0001.png output/lipsync/0001.png' used to fake the rendering process.

integrity.description: The output files are the result of a genuine OpenToonz render.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of OpenToonz render dialogs or the OpenToonz main window being used to initiate the render process (e.g., clicking 'Render' or 'Fast Render').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source assets in '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/mouth_shapes' are synthetic proxy images consisting of white rectangles of increasing size. The final rendered output must contain at least 30 PNG frames in '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/lipsync'. If you inspect the output frames, frame 5, frame 15, and frame 25 should show progressively larger white rectangles (ascending pixel mass/brightness), corresponding to Drawing 1, Drawing 2, and Drawing 3 respectively.
